canonical_params.expression: coerce sig_id, cp_type, cp_time, cp_dose, g-* and c-* into one numeric matrix, then fit StandardScaler on train and transform train/test

actions.reasoning: Standard-scaling identifiers and categorical treatment metadata together with assay values requires arbitrary numeric codes and contaminates the feature matrix before the observed RankGauss/PCA path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Misapplies the numeric normalization step to identifier and categorical columns that should instead be dropped or encoded.

actions.reasoning: Fitting separate PCA bases for train and test creates incompatible component spaces, so a model trained on train components cannot be applied coherently to test components.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Uses the observed gene PCA family but fits independent train/test component bases instead of one shared basis.

canonical_params.expression: for each g-* and c-* column, fit QuantileTransformer(n_quantiles=100, output_distribution='normal') on train and transform train/test

actions.reasoning: RankGauss normalization makes the high-dimensional gene and cell distributions better conditioned for PCA and neural models.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 11 of 30 notebooks and repeatedly used in the strongest PCA/NN pipelines.

actions.reasoning: Winsorizing every assay column to its interquartile range before RankGauss collapses half of each feature's ranked response distribution and destroys tail signal needed by PCA and neural models.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Destructive quantile clipping applied before the rank-based normalization step.

canonical_params.expression: fit separate KMeans models on g-* and c-* groups over the shared train/test feature frame; append one-hot cluster assignments

actions.reasoning: KMeans cluster assignments over gene and cell groups capture neighborhood structure in some extended LISH-MoA feature-engineering pipelines.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 4 of 30 notebooks. Kept for all-scope evaluation only because it is uncommon and model/pipeline-specific.

actions.reasoning: Reducing 772 gene features to only five replacement components destroys most gene-expression signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Uses the correct feature family but an excessively lossy component count and replacement mode.

actions.reasoning: This reverses the treatment-time order and encodes only cp_time, leaving dose unhandled.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Perturbs the ordinal categorical mapping by reversing time semantics and omitting dose.

actions.reasoning: sig_id is a unique string sample identifier and should not be used as a model feature.
actions.context_notes: All 30 reviewed notebooks exclude sig_id before constructing the feature matrix.
evidence_snippets: feature_cols = [c for c in feature_cols if c not in ['kfold','sig_id']]

actions.reasoning: One-hot encoding sig_id creates a very wide near-unique indicator matrix with no reusable signal for unseen test ids.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Applies categorical encoding to the identifier column that should be dropped.

actions.reasoning: Cell viability columns are a biologically distinct signal group; dropping them loses response information used in the separate PCA path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Destructive feature-group drop based on the known LISH-MoA column families.

actions.reasoning: Encoding only dose leaves treatment duration as an unhandled categorical feature or discards it later.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Partial implementation of the one-hot treatment encoding family.

actions.reasoning: After control rows are removed from training, cp_type has one remaining value and carries no discriminative signal.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 15 of 30 notebooks. Must happen after the control-row filter if cp_type is needed to identify controls.

actions.reasoning: Replacing 100 cell-viability columns with only two components throws away most of the cell-response signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Uses the right cell feature group but an excessively lossy component count and replacement mode.

actions.reasoning: A single combined PCA mixes biologically distinct gene expression and cell viability spaces, losing the structure preserved by separate decompositions.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Collapses the two observed PCA families into one combined PCA basis.

actions.reasoning: Randomly retaining only 1,866 rows, roughly the control-row count, discards more than 90% of the labeled training data and makes rare MoA targets unstable or absent.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Recasts control-count matching as an invalid row-sampling strategy for a sparse multilabel task.

canonical_params.expression: fit out-of-fold target encoders for sig_id against the 206 scored MoA targets and append the encoded columns

actions.reasoning: Target encoding the near-unique sig_id key memorizes training samples and cannot generalize to unseen test identifiers.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Applies supervised encoding to the identifier column that should be dropped.

canonical_params.scoring: 206 scored MoA targets including unavailable test labels

actions.reasoning: Supervised feature selection cannot use test targets; describing it as train_and_test creates impossible leakage.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Turns unsupervised variance filtering into an invalid supervised selector over unavailable test labels.

actions.reasoning: Filtering rows based on all gene-expression values being nonnegative removes most valid assay rows and biases the training set.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Generic outlier-row filter that is invalid for signed gene expression measurements.

canonical_params.expression: fit KMeans on sig_id, cp_type, cp_time, cp_dose, g-* and c-* after coercing identifiers/categories to numeric codes

actions.reasoning: Clustering after mixing identifiers and categorical treatment metadata with assay features makes Euclidean cluster assignments depend on arbitrary codes rather than biological response structure.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Applies the observed KMeans feature idea to an invalid mixed column set rather than separate numeric assay groups.

actions.reasoning: Treatment duration and dose are categorical descriptors that must be converted to numeric features before modeling.
actions.context_notes: One-hot encoding is the most common treatment-metadata strategy, observed in 19 of 30 reviewed notebooks.

actions.reasoning: Binary quantile binning converts rich expression and viability measurements into coarse signs, throwing away most magnitude information.
actions.derivation_reasoning: A more destructive discretization distractor for the same numeric feature groups.

actions.reasoning: This keeps only control vehicle rows, which have all-zero targets, and discards the compound-treated rows that contain the actual MoA signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Predicate inversion of the canonical control-row exclusion.

actions.reasoning: Gene expression columns are the largest signal source in the competition; dropping them leaves only metadata and cell features.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Destructive feature-group drop based on the known LISH-MoA column families.

actions.reasoning: Aggressively clipping assay values to [-1, 1] truncates biological response magnitude before normalization/PCA.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Fixed-range clipping that truncates assay magnitudes before normalization and dimensionality reduction.

canonical_params.expression: fit separate QuantileTransformer(output_distribution='normal') objects on train and test for every g-* and c-* column

actions.reasoning: Fitting separate quantile transforms for train and test changes feature scales independently and makes test features incomparable to the training representation.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Uses the observed RankGauss method but with inconsistent split-wise fitting.

actions.reasoning: VarianceThreshold removes near-constant numeric features and low-variance PCA components that add noise without signal.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 15 of 30 notebooks. The canonical threshold 0.8 follows strong Kushal/VBMokin/Riad-style pipelines.

canonical_params.expression: for each row, compute mean/std over sig_id, cp_type, cp_time, cp_dose, g-* and c-* together

actions.reasoning: Aggregating over identifiers and categorical metadata together with numeric assay features produces meaningless row summaries.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Applies the observed row-statistics idea to the wrong column set.

actions.reasoning: Yeo-Johnson is a numeric power transform; applying it to sig_id and categorical treatment descriptors after arbitrary coding distorts nominal categories and reintroduces the sample identifier as a feature.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Misapplies a numeric transform to columns that should be dropped or categorically encoded.

actions.reasoning: Raw gene and cell values include negative measurements, so log1p with an offset of 1 can create invalid values before modeling.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Substitutes a generic skew transform for the observed rank-based normalization.

canonical_params.expression: for each g-* and c-* column, fit QuantileTransformer(n_quantiles=100, output_distribution='uniform') on train and transform train/test

actions.reasoning: Uniform quantile scaling is not RankGauss and undermines the normal-distribution assumption used by the PCA/NN feature-engineering path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Changes only the QuantileTransformer output distribution from normal to uniform.

actions.reasoning: Ordinal numeric mapping is a common alternative to one-hot encoding for treatment duration and dose.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 12 of 30 notebooks. It satisfies the same preprocessing need as one-hot encoding and is therefore one equivalence unit with CA-000020.

canonical_params.expression: replace cp_time/cp_dose with full-training target means for the 206 scored targets without out-of-fold separation

actions.reasoning: Target encoding treatment metadata without out-of-fold safeguards leaks label information into the training features.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Substitutes a leaky supervised encoding for the observed unsupervised categorical encodings.

actions.reasoning: Dropping treatment duration and dose without encoding them discards predictive experimental metadata used by most strong notebooks.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Replaces the categorical treatment encoding family with a destructive drop.

canonical_params.predicate: cp_type != 'ctl_vehicle' on training rows only; preserve all test rows for submission alignment

actions.reasoning: Control vehicle samples have all-zero MoA targets by experimental design; filtering them from training avoids teaching the model that drug-response rows should look like controls.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 24 of 30 reviewed notebooks. Applied to training rows; test controls are usually retained for submission and assigned near-zero predictions.

actions.reasoning: Keeping only a 6% stratified sample by cp_type discards most labeled MoA examples while preserving only the treatment/control ratio, not the rare multilabel target coverage.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Misapplies class-balancing intuition to a perturbation-type column where controls are not a target class.

actions.reasoning: Keeping only one treatment duration discards valid 48h and 72h experiments and narrows the training distribution.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Mistakes a treatment descriptor for a row-quality filter.

actions.reasoning: Keeping only the 20 highest-variance raw assay features before PCA discards almost all gene and cell measurements and prevents the observed dimensionality-reduction path from recovering broad response structure.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Turns the observed low-variance cleanup step into an aggressive top-k raw-feature selector.

actions.reasoning: Box-Cox requires positive inputs and is unsuitable for raw expression/viability features with negative values.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Uses a power transform that violates the LISH-MoA numeric feature domain.

canonical_params.expression: for each row, compute sum/mean/std/kurtosis/skew over g-* and c-* feature groups

actions.reasoning: Row-level distribution statistics summarize each sample's gene and cell response profile and appear in a smaller set of extended feature-engineering notebooks.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 6 of 30 notebooks. Kept for all-scope evaluation but not primary core because it is optional extended feature engineering.

canonical_params.expression: fit separate VarianceThreshold selectors on train and test numeric features and keep each split's selected columns independently

actions.reasoning: Fitting separate selectors for train and test can produce different feature spaces, breaking model application to test rows.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Applies the right threshold idea but independently per split.

actions.reasoning: Uniform binning discards continuous assay resolution across hundreds of dense biological features.
actions.derivation_reasoning: A generic numeric discretization distractor for high-dimensional continuous assay data.

actions.reasoning: Taking logs of absolute expression values destroys sign information that is meaningful in gene expression responses.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Another generic transform distractor that damages the assay signal.

actions.reasoning: Separate PCA on gene expression features captures dense gene-response components without mixing them with cell-viability measurements.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 13 of 30 notebooks. Component counts vary, but the canonical high-capacity variant follows strong Kushal/VBMokin-style notebooks.

actions.reasoning: Encoding only treatment time leaves dose as an unhandled categorical feature or discards it later.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Partial implementation of the one-hot treatment encoding family.

actions.reasoning: Keeping only the D1 dose subset discards valid D2 compound-treatment rows and biases the learned MoA response toward one treatment-dose condition.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Mistakes dose metadata handling for row filtering, replacing categorical treatment encoding with a destructive dose-only subset.

actions.reasoning: Crossing treatment metadata with every assay column explodes the feature space without the notebook evidence supporting such a design.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Generic interaction-feature distractor for already high-dimensional assay data.

actions.reasoning: Separate PCA on cell viability features preserves the distinction between cell-health and gene-expression response spaces.
actions.context_notes: Observed in 13 of 30 notebooks, usually paired with gene PCA while keeping the two feature groups separate.

actions.reasoning: All pairwise interactions among 772 gene columns create hundreds of thousands of features and overwhelm the modest row count.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Generic feature-crossing distractor that is unsuitable at LISH-MoA dimensionality.

actions.reasoning: Dropping cp_type before control filtering makes it impossible to identify the ctl_vehicle training rows that should be removed.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Uses the right column but at the wrong point in the preprocessing order.

actions.reasoning: Threshold 2.0 is too aggressive after RankGauss/PCA-style processing and removes too much signal.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Over-tightens the observed VarianceThreshold into a destructive selector.

actions.reasoning: Standard scaling is mechanically applicable to the assay matrix, but it is a generic replacement for the observed RankGauss normalization path and does not preserve the rank-normal feature distribution used by strong LISH-MoA PCA/NN pipelines.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Covers the numeric scaling family as a plausible but non-observed normalization alternative to RankGauss.

actions.reasoning: Min-max scaling compresses each assay feature into a bounded range but remains a generic scaling substitute rather than the observed RankGauss/PCA preprocessing path for LISH-MoA.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second numeric scaling distractor so SCALE_NUMERIC is represented by at least two nearby rows rather than a singleton coverage hint.
